brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is an analytic factorization of a Selberg zeta function into a Weierstrass product over Laplace resonances (spectral/geometric divisor of an automorphic zeta function), whereas Paper B's machinery is a hybrid quantum-classical feedback protocol that uses classical optimization onto a low-entanglement variational manifold to stabilize regular trajectories in a chaotic many-body system. There is no role-for-role correspondence: one is a static complex-analytic product formula, the other an iterative dynamical control/optimization loop over phase space, so they share no transferable underlying method beyond the generic word 'spectral/dynamics'.
